0001.description: Platform reset line stayed asserted during boot; clear CMOS and retry
repairs: 1. Remove all power resources (the ac power adapter, removable battery, and coin-cell battery). If your computer has a built-in battery, reset the computer by inserting a straightened paper clip into the emergency-reset hole. Wait for one minute. Then reconnect all power resources. 2. Replace the system board (service provider only).

0002.description: Internal chipset bus failed a self-test
repairs: Replace the system board (service provider only).

0281.description: Embedded controller reported a generic fault
repairs: Replace the system board (service provider only).

0282.description: Memory module failed self-test; reseat DIMMs
repairs: 1. Reinstall or replace the memory module. 2. Replace the system board (service provider only).
1. Remove PCIe devices (the M.2 card, PCIe card, and so on) (service provider only). 2. Replace the system board (service provider only).

0284.description: TCG subsystem fault; likely a BIOS code-integrity check failure
repairs: Replace the system board (service provider only).

0285.description: TCG subsystem fault; likely a TPM initialisation failure
repairs: Replace the system board (service provider only).
Replace the system board (service provider only).
1. Reinstall or replace the discrete graphics card (service provider only). 2. Replace the system board (service provider only).
1. Reconnect the display cable on both the system board side and the computer display side (service provider only). 2. Replace the system board (service provider only).

B0xx.description: System halted during POST; the last two hex digits identify the POST stage
repairs: Unplug the system, wait 30 seconds, plug in the system, and power up.
Check diagnostic log for additional alerts, such as power, voltage, or fan alerts. Follow that repair action.
Remove any devices (adapters, memory, etc.) installed after the system was received from Lenovo.

Remove non-essential I/O such as USB keys. Remove non-essential devices, reducing to minimize memory and adapters. If the diagnostic alert is resolved, add back devices one at a time to determine problem device.
Contact Lenovo service.
sources: For B0##, the ## represents the BIOS port 80 checkpoint code
Codes are sometimes redundant between different suppliers of ThinkStation BIOS - AMI, Intel, ROM vendors
At this time, we do not have a compiled list of checkpoint codes to offer

B201.description: Intel Management Engine reported a warning
sources: This warning should not impact the stability of the system. If the warning occurs multiple time within a short period, follow the suggested actions for code B202.

B202.description: Intel Management Engine reported an error
repairs: Unplug the system, wait 30 seconds, plug in the system, and power up.
Check diagnostic log for additional alerts, such as power, voltage, or fan alerts. Follow that repair action.
Contact Lenovo service.
sources: Intel(R) Management Engine has reported a error

B2Fx.description: Intel Management Engine reset; the last hex digit identifies the reason

B4FF.description: Simulated critical error (test harness only; not a real fault)
repairs: No action required.
If this were a real event, suggested actions to resolve the event would be listed in this section.
sources: This error is a simulated event through software.
Hardware is not affected.
If this were a real event, potential sources for the event would be listed in this section.

B4x0.description: Option ROM failed to initialise; the second hex digit is the slot
repairs: Unplug the system, wait 30 seconds, plug in the system, and power up.
Check diagnostic log for additional alerts, such as power, voltage, or fan alerts. Follow that repair action.
Remove any devices (adapters, memory, etc.) installed after the system was received from Lenovo.
If the device listed in error title remains in the system, remove the device, or disable in BIOS setup.
Contact Lenovo service.
sources: The firmware for the device listed in the title has reported an error

B500.description: Recorded model or serial number has changed since last boot

B501.description: Installed hardware may draw more than the power supply can deliver

B69x.description: Faulty memory row on a CPU 1 DIMM was not repaired; the last hex digit is the DIMM slot

B6Ax.description: Faulty memory row on a CPU 2 DIMM was not repaired; the last hex digit is the DIMM slot

B6xx.description: A faulty memory row was repaired in place

B7xx.description: Memory subsystem warning; the last two hex digits identify the specific fault
sources: This warning should not impact the stability of the system. If the warning occurs multiple time within a short period, follow the suggested actions for code B8xx.

B8xx.description: Critical memory error; system cannot continue
repairs: Unplug the system, wait 30 seconds, plug in the system, and power up.
Check for foreign objects on electronic components in the system.
Remove and reinstall all DIMMs to ensure good connection.
Remove all but one DIMM. If diagnostic alert continues, swap with another DIMM. Add back DIMMs one at a time to determine problem DIMM.
Swap CPU1 and CPU2. If only one CPU, reseat the CPU.  WARNING: Care must be taken when removing/addings CPUs.  If not familiar with this process, skip this step.
Contact Lenovo service.
sources: The memory handler has reported a critical error to BIOS
Defective DIMM or poor DIMM connection.
Unexpected SW/HW malfunction.

C000.description: Chassis intrusion detected: the system cover was opened

C100.description: Backplane S/X cable is connected incorrectly

C31x.description: Backplane P1 or P2 cable is connected incorrectly; the last hex digit is the drive index
repairs: The "P1" and "P2" cables may only be connected to Broadcom storage adapters.
The Broadcom adapter has four connectors: 0, 1, 2, 3.  "P1" must be connected to connector 0 or 2.  "P2" must be connected to connector immediately after "P1" - 1 or 3.
sources: The blind connect cables labeled "P1" and "P2" have been detected in unsupported connections

C41x.description: Backplane P1 or P2 cable is connected incorrectly; the last hex digit is the drive index
repairs: The "P1" and "P2" cables may only be connected to Broadcom storage adapters.
The Broadcom adapter has four connectors: 0, 1, 2, 3.  "P1" must be connected to connector 0 or 2.  "P2" must be connected to connector immediately after "P1" - 1 or 3.
sources: The blind connect cables labeled "P1" and "P2" have been detected in unsupported connections

C500.description: Could not read backplane cabling configuration

D11x.description: U.2 PCIe SSD is installed in the wrong slot; the last hex digit is the drive index
repairs: For P920, flip the tray so that the U.2 drive is on the bottom.  Install first U.2 in bay 4, then the next in bay 3, bay 2, and finally bay 1.
For P720, flip the tray so that the U.2 drive is on the top.  Install first U.2 in bay 1, then the 2nd drive in bay 2.
sources: The system has determined the 2.5 inch PCIe U.2 drive is installed in a port that does not support PCIe

F110.description: CPU 1 fan is running outside its expected speed range
sources: This warning should not impact the stability of the system. If the warning occurs multiple time within a short period, follow the suggested actions for code F111.

repairs: Check for foreign object in fan.
Check that all fans are connected.
Check diagnostic log for additional alerts, such as power, voltage, or fan alerts. Follow that repair action.
Contact Lenovo service.
sources: Power to the fan is abnormal.
A foreign object may be blocking or slowing the fan.

P100.description: AC input voltage is below tolerance
sources: This warning should not impact the stability of the system. If the warning occurs multiple time within a short period, follow the suggested actions for code P101.

P101.description: AC input voltage is critically low; system may shut down
repairs: Check the power cord, outlet, and circuit breaker.
Try a different outlet.
If the power circuit is subject to regular disturbance (brown-out), consider a battery back-up unit.
Contact Lenovo service.
sources: Circuit line disturbance (brown-out).
Power supply components wear.

P110.description: A power-supply output rail is critically low
repairs: Remove any devices (adapters, memory, etc.) installed after the system was received from Lenovo.

Remove non-essential I/O such as USB keys.  Remove non-essential devices, reducing to minimize memory and adapters.  If the diagnostic alert is resolved, add back devices one at a time to determine problem device.
Contact Lenovo service.
sources: System device (adapters, memory, etc.) drawing too much power.
Power supply components wear.

Remove non-essential I/O such as USB keys. Remove non-essential devices, reducing to minimize memory and adapters. If the diagnostic alert is resolved, add back devices one at a time to determine problem device.
Contact Lenovo service.

repairs: Unplug the system, wait 30 seconds, plug in the system, and power up.
Check for foreign objects on electronic components in the system.

Remove non-essential I/O such as USB keys. Remove non-essential devices, reducing to minimize memory and adapters. If the diagnostic alert is resolved, add back devices one at a time to determine problem device.
Contact Lenovo service.

S033.description: CPU voltage rail failed to come up
repairs: Unplug the system, wait 30 seconds, plug in the system, and power up.
Check for foreign objects on electronic components in the system.
Swap CPU1 and CPU2. If only one CPU, reseat the CPU.  WARNING: Care must be taken when removing/addings CPUs.  If not familiar with this process, skip this step.
Contact Lenovo service.

S050.description: An external device is drawing leakage current
repairs: Replace the display port cable between system and monitor with a cable that meets VESA specifications.  The cable that shipped with your monitor should meet specifications.
sources: Display port cable does not meet VESA specification, driving power back into system.  This source may cause the diagnostic system to report other errors.

S201.description: System recovered from a general alert

sources: This warning should not impact the stability of the system. If the warning occurs multiple time within a short period, follow the suggested actions for code T112.

T111.description: CPU 1 reached its maximum allowed temperature
sources: This warning should not impact the stability of the system. If the warning occurs multiple time within a short period, follow the suggested actions for code T112.

T112.description: CPU 1 thermal limit exceeded; throttling or shutdown imminent
repairs: Check for ventilation clearance around system. Remove any dust of objects blocking air flow.
Check the ambient temperature in the room is within specified limits, typically 10-35 degrees celsius.
Power the system down for serveral minutes, then power on and check for the error again.
Contact Lenovo service.
sources: The system does not have appropriate clearence for ventilation.

sources: This warning should not impact the stability of the system. If the warning occurs multiple time within a short period, follow the suggested actions for code T7x6.

T7x5.description: Quad M.2 adapter card 1 thermal limit exceeded; the second hex digit is the drive slot
repairs: Check for ventilation clearance around system. Remove any dust of objects blocking air flow.
Check the ambient temperature in the room is within specified limits, typically 10-35 degrees celsius.
Remove the quad M.2 adapter from the system.  Remove the cover and heatsink.  Confirm thermal pads are in place and making good connection between the heatsink and M.2
Check diagnostic log for additional alerts, such as power, voltage, or fan alerts. Follow that repair action.
Check that all fans are connected.
Reboot system, enter system setup, and increase the fan speed to setting 2 or 3.
Contact Lenovo service.
sources: The system does not have appropriate clearence for ventilation.
The temperature in the room is beyond specified limits.
A foreign object may be blocking or slowing the fan.
Power to the fan is abnormal.
The quad M.2 adapter heatsink is not performing as expected.

T7x6.description: Quad M.2 adapter card 2 thermal limit exceeded; the second hex digit is the drive slot
repairs: Check for ventilation clearance around system. Remove any dust of objects blocking air flow.
Check the ambient temperature in the room is within specified limits, typically 10-35 degrees celsius.
Remove the quad M.2 adapter from the system.  Remove the cover and heatsink.  Confirm thermal pads are in place and making good connection between the heatsink and M.2
Check diagnostic log for additional alerts, such as power, voltage, or fan alerts. Follow that repair action.
Check that all fans are connected.
Reboot system, enter system setup, and increase the fan speed to setting 2 or 3.
Contact Lenovo service.

sources: The system does not have appropriate clearence for ventilation.
The temperature in the room is beyond specified limits.
A foreign object may be blocking or slowing the fan.
Power to the fan is abnormal.
The quad M.2 adapter heatsink is not performing as expected.

repairs: Unplug the system, wait 30 seconds, plug in the system, and power up.
Download and run the 'Lenovo Super IO and Front Panel Firmware update utility'. This can be found on Lenovo's website in your system's Drivers and Software page.
Contact Lenovo service.
sources: Communication issue between motherboard and front panel IO cable.
Embedded Super IO controller, or front panel IO USB hub firmware is downlevel.

U110.description: Front-panel charging port is drawing high current
sources: This warning should not impact the stability of the system. If the warning occurs multiple time within a short period, follow the suggested actions for code U111.

U111.description: Front-panel charging port is drawing critical over-current
repairs: Remove device from front USB port.
sources: A device in a USB port may be consuming too much power.

U120.description: Front-panel non-charging ports are drawing high current
sources: This warning should not impact the stability of the system. If the warning occurs multiple time within a short period, follow the suggested actions for code U121.

U121.description: Front-panel non-charging ports are drawing critical over-current
repairs: Remove device from front USB port.
sources: A device in a USB port may be consuming too much power.

U130.description: Front-panel USB bus is drawing high current
sources: This warning should not impact the stability of the system. If the warning occurs multiple time within a short period, follow the suggested actions for code U121.
